name: Sierra del Carche and Snow Well Loop
name_original: Pinoso, Pozo de la Nieve, Raspay – Ruta Circular – Sierra del Carche
description: This difficult 21.3-mile mountain biking route in Sierra del Carche offers challenging terrain, great views, and takes you past the Snow Wel
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Pinós offer diverse landscapes, from dense pine forests to expansive vineyards and almond groves. This region, located inland from Alicante, Spain, features significant elevation changes, with routes traversing mountainous areas and agricultural vistas. The varied terrain provides a compelling environment for mountain biking, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around El Pinós The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra del Carche and Snow Well Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete, featuring challenging climbs and mountain views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trails in El Molinet – Hermitage of Saint Barbara loop from el Pinós / Pinoso, a difficult 43.9 miles (70.8 km) path that explores diverse landscapes with significant elevation gains. Local mountain bikers also love the Narrow Streets of Pinoso – Telecommunications Towers loop from el Pinós / Pinoso, a 23.5 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around El Pinós is defined by pine forests, agricultural vistas, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with over 1,000 meters of ascent. The routes in El Pinós are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Pinós's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in El Pinós?
A: El Pinós offers a comprehensive network of over 30 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging rides with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Pinós?
A: The terrain in El Pinós is diverse, featuring dense pine forests, expansive vineyards, and picturesque almond and olive groves. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, from smooth singletracks to more rugged paths, often with challenging climbs and descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in El Pinós?
A: Yes, El Pinós has options for all skill levels. There are 5 easy routes available, offering gentler terrain and shorter distances, which can be suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. These trails allow you to enjoy the scenic agricultural vistas.

Q: What are some unique natural features or landmarks I might see along the trails?
A: El Pinós is rich in natural and geological features. You might encounter the unique gypsum and salt mountain of Cabeso del Sal, or explore areas with remains of gypsum ovens and caves. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Lagrimal&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1050338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Roja&lt;/a&gt;, both natural monuments in the area.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Pinós?
A: Yes, many routes in El Pinós are designed as loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933437530/sierra-del-carche-and-snow-well-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Carche and Snow Well Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers mountain views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31422714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trails in El Molinet – Hermitage of Saint Barbara loop&lt;/a&gt; explores diverse landscapes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Pinós?
A: The region is particularly picturesque in late winter when the almond trees are in bloom, adding a beautiful backdrop to your rides. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in El Pinós?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Pinós are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging pine forests to scenic agricultural vistas, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. El Pinós offers 8 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933437530/sierra-del-carche-and-snow-well-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Carche and Snow Well Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31422714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trails in El Molinet – Hermitage of Saint Barbara loop&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1,000 meters of ascent and descent, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: Can I find bike rental services in or near El Pinós?
A: Yes, bike rental services are available in the area, including regular, electric, and electric mountain bikes. This makes it convenient for visitors who prefer not to bring their own equipment.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike routes?
A: Many trails in El Pinós offer scenic viewpoints, especially those traversing mountainous areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10329528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narrow Streets of Pinoso – Telecommunications Towers loop&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas. You can also find panoramic views from summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Carche Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geodetic Vertex of La Capilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in the El Pinós region besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, El Pinós and its surroundings are excellent for a variety of outdoor activities. You can enjoy hiking, horseback riding, and even rafting on the Segura River. The natural parks and landscapes are ideal for exploring on foot or by other means.
tips: This monument, with the name of "Coliseum Crema Marfil del Vinalopó", is built at the access to Pinoso, on the regional road that joins the cities of Elda and Jumilla. It is a roundabout of 1,100 tons of ivory cream marble, which serves as a tribute to all those people who have dedicated their lives to the natural stone sector.
Great view of the mountain and the slightly uphill path.

This snow well is a construction of traditional architecture with great ethnographic importance. Its construction began in 1683. The snow/ice was stored for later transport to nearby towns, intended for use both in food preservation and in the field of medicine.
Beautiful views over small terraced fields.
Raspay belongs to the municipality of Yecla, Region of Murcia. It has several places of interest, among which the Zurriera cave, Jaime el Barbudo and the Pansas fountain stand out.

faqs.question: What is the technical difficulty of the Pinoso, Pozo de la Nieve, Raspay mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging mountain biking route, rated as 'difficult' and corresponding to STS S3 - S6 on the Single Track Scale. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for sections where pushing their bike might be necessary due to the technical nature of the terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse range of surfaces. You'll encounter significant stretches of street (20.2 km), as well as paths (6.55 km) and singletrack (4.31 km). There are also portions of paved road (3.07 km), asphalt (1.17 km), unpaved sections (10.7 km), and a small segment of alpine terrain (138 m). This mix ensures a dynamic and engaging ride.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Pinoso, Pozo de la Nieve, Raspay route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the beautiful Sierra del Carche Regional Park. Along the way, you can see the Coliseum Crema Marfil del Vinalopó, enjoy Great views of the mountains, and visit the historic Snow Well of the Sierra del Carche. You'll also get Views of the Terraced Fields in Sierra del Carche Regional Park and pass through Raspay Village.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, given the trail's location in the Sierra del Carche, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the Pinoso, Pozo de la Nieve, Raspay – Ruta Circular – Sierra del Carche trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog rules for this particular mountain bike route is not available. However, as it passes through the Parque Regional de Sierra del Carche, it's advisable to check the regional park's official regulations regarding dogs, especially leash requirements, to ensure compliance and safety for all users and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra del Carche Regional Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Parque Regional de Sierra del Carche for recreational activities like mountain biking. Permits are typically not required for general trail use, but it's always a good practice to check the official website of the regional park for any updated regulations or specific requirements for certain activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting and ending near Pinoso. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking options within Pinoso or at designated access points to the Sierra del Carche Regional Park that are convenient for starting the loop. Local signage or online maps may provide more precise parking locations.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this mountain bike route overlap with several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of El Camí IT5TR3 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (12% of the route), Transmurciana 26 (12%), Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós (3%), European long distance path E4 - part Spain (2%), GR 7: Andorra - Strait of Gibraltar (2%), Camí de la Capellania (11%), and Senda al Pozo de Nieve (2%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, gloves, and protective eyewear. Carry plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Due to potential remote sections, consider a fully charged phone and a power bank.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Pinoso, a town where you can expect to find various facilities. Pinoso offers options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation, which can be convenient for pre- or post-ride refreshments and stays. Raspay Village, another point on the loop, is a smaller settlement that might have limited services.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for electric mountain bikes (e-MTBs)?
faqs.answer: While the trail is technically demanding, its length and significant elevation gain (778m up) make it a good candidate for e-MTBs, especially for riders who want assistance on the climbs or to extend their range. However, the technical sections (STS S3-S6) still require advanced riding skills regardless of the bike type.
